name: Khabib Nurmagomedov

qualityWinLedger.context: UFC two-division champion and elite lightweight title opponent.
Prime elite lightweight and future UFC champion.
Dangerous top lightweight contender in a deep division.
Strong ranked lightweight win with prime-speed danger.
Short-notice vacant-title opponent, useful but not elite.
Physically difficult early UFC win; close-fight context flagged.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Retired undefeated in UFC after a title defense.

shortCase: Khabib is the cleanest peak-dominance case: unbeaten, overwhelming, and almost never in real trouble once he reached elite lightweight level.
bestArgument: Khabib’s best argument is purity: no UFC losses, no real collapse, and a final stretch that looked like total separation from elite lightweights.
counterArgument: Khabib’s argument against almost anyone is purity. No losses, no real collapse, no post-prime damage, and one of the clearest primes ever.
whyRankedHere: Khabib lands here because his prime-dominance score is the strongest in the current scoring model. He combined elite control, round winning, and a perfect UFC record, giving him one of the hardest peaks to challenge in this ranking.
whyNotHigher: He does not climb higher because the current scoring model gives him less championship volume and fewer quality-wins layers than the fighters above him. His peak is elite enough to compete with anyone, but his total UFC resume is shorter.
finalTakeaway: Khabib is the lightweight prime-dominance benchmark: unbeatable at his best, extremely efficient, and held back only by shorter championship volume than the names above him.
